items.headline: Apple shares rise as iPhone 17 and MacBook Neo drive forecast
items.summary: STORY: Apple on Thursday touted blowout demand for its flagship iPhone 17 and the MacBook Neo that helped power a solid sales forecast sending its shares up nearly 4% after hours.The tech giant's executives said they expect sales growth of up to 17% in the current fiscal third quarter.That's above Wall Street estimates of 9.5%, according to LSEG data.In the second quarter ended March 28, sales and profits were at around $111 billion and $2 per share, above analyst expectations.In the latest quarter, sales of the iPhone, still the company's best-selling product, were $56.9 billion, slightly less than estimates.CEO Tim Cook said iPhone sales were held back in the quarter by supply constraints for the device's advanced processor chips.Mac sales, which included several weeks of Neo sales, were $8.4 billion, beating expectations.Analysts believe the Neo could help Apple crack a new $20 billion market for lower-priced laptops.In the fiscal second quarter, Apple’s existing inventory of memory chips helped it weather rising prices.But memory costs will catch up to Apple starting in the current quarter ending in June.Cook, in a conference call with analysts, said they believe it will "drive an increasing impact" on the business.

items.headline: Apple Q2 Earnings Call Highlights
items.summary: Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) used its fiscal second-quarter 2026 earnings call to report double-digit growth, record March-quarter revenue, and provide more detail on supply constraints and rising memory costs, while also highlighting a major leadership transition set for later this year. items.headline: Stock Market Today, April 30: Aurora Innovation Jumps on Hirschbach’s 500-Truck Autonomous Freight Plan
items.summary: Aurora’s Hirschbach plan gives investors a clearer look at how autonomous freight could move toward commercial scale, but the stock’s rally still depends on how quickly it moves from planned deployment to revenue.

items.headline: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures tick up after gauges close April on record highs
items.summary: Stock futures climbed following record closes after sweltering Big Tech earnings in a volatile market.

items.headline: Coastal Pay Launches SignUp Link—Two-Minute, Friction-Free Merchant Onboarding for Retail and E-Commerce

items.headline: ServiceNow And Simplify Alpha Target Health Plan Costs With AI Workflows
items.summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) has entered a partnership with Simplify Alpha to automate and modernize core U.S. health plan operations using AI powered workflow solutions. The collaboration targets key functions such as claims processing, appeals and grievances, provider network management, and sales operations. The solutions are designed to be production ready, auditable, and scalable for healthcare payers facing rising regulatory and cost pressures. ServiceNow comes into this partnership with a...
